description: Phase 3: Full game state persistence (save/load/auto-save) routed through tRPC gameRouter (no browser-direct DB), versioned save format with Zod validation and migrations, and a comprehensive debug menu gated to NODE_ENV=development. Depends on PRD-01 and PRD-02.

testingDirectives.framework: Vitest (already configured at vitest.config.ts, run via `pnpm test`)
requirements: Every new module MUST have a co-located test file (e.g., GameState.ts -> src/game/__tests__/engine/GameState.test.ts). Story is not complete without tests.
Unit tests are the minimum quality bar. `pnpm test` must pass before committing.
The full verification command is: `pnpm test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.

testPatterns.stateModules: Test all public methods: get/set/serialize/restore. Verify onChange callbacks fire. Verify invalid inputs are rejected. Use the existing GameState.test.ts as a pattern.
testPatterns.persistence: Mock the tRPC adapter (vi.fn()). Test save/load/migrate flows without network. Verify dirty tracking, auto-save timing, and error handling.
testPatterns.migrations: Test each migration transform with fixture data. Verify unknown versions throw. Test full migration chain (0.1 -> latest).
testPatterns.debugMenu: Test that debug value changes propagate through GameState/runtimeConfig. Test that debug state is excluded from serialization.

existingCodeContext.gameState: src/game/engine/GameState.ts already has yarn (get/set/addYarn/deductYarn/onYarnChange) and inventory (addResource/hasInventorySpace/onInventoryChange) with reactive listener Sets. US-201 must extend this, not replace it -- preserve existing convenience methods as wrappers over the new path-based API so callers in Game.ts, systems, and HUD don't break.
existingCodeContext.gameRouter: src/server/api/routers/game.ts already has getSave/upsertSave/deleteSave procedures. upsertSave currently accepts Record<string, unknown> for saveData -- US-202 should tighten this with the Zod schema.
existingCodeContext.gameTrpcAdapter: GameCanvas.tsx creates a GameTrpcAdapter passed to Game constructor (opts.trpc). Currently only has upsertSave(). US-203 needs to extend it with getSave() for the load flow.
existingCodeContext.debugBridge: window.__catHerderDebug is already exposed in Game.ts (dev-only). Provides world, gameState, eventBus, physicsEngine, getPlayerEntity(), getActiveCats(), tick(n).
existingCodeContext.hudPattern: HUD.ts builds DOM elements in constructor, updates via setX() methods called from UIManager. UIManager.update() receives HUDState from Game.buildHUDState(). Follow this pattern for save indicator.
existingCodeContext.config: src/game/config.ts already has autoSaveIntervalMs: 30000 -- use this for auto-save timing.
existingCodeContext.eventBus: src/game/engine/EventBus.ts -- used throughout for decoupled communication. Emit SAVE_FAILED, DEBUG_VALUE_CHANGED, etc. here.

testingDirectives.debugBridge: window.__catHerderDebug is exposed in dev mode (Game.ts). It provides: world, gameState, eventBus, physicsEngine, getPlayerEntity(), getActiveCats(), tick(n) for deterministic physics stepping. E2E scripts can use this via dev-browser.
antiPatterns: Do NOT mark a story as passing without `pnpm test` succeeding.
Do NOT write tests that import Three.js or require DOM -- use mocks for SceneManager/InputManager.
Do NOT test implementation details (private methods) -- test behavior through public APIs.
Do NOT skip tests to make CI green -- fix the underlying issue.
Do NOT replace existing GameState methods -- extend GameState with the path-based API and keep existing methods as wrappers.
Do NOT use import.meta.env.DEV in Next.js -- use process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' for dev-only gating.

userStories.title: GameState full implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a central state store that tracks all game data with change detection so UI updates reactively and serialization captures everything.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend src/game/engine/GameState.ts with path-based API: get<T>(path: string): T, set<T>(path: string, value: T), onChange<T>(path: string, callback: (newVal: T, oldVal: T) => void): Unsubscribe, serialize(): SaveData, restore(data: SaveData).

Internal state object includes: player (appearance, position {x,y,z}, stats {level, health, maxHealth}, yarn, oxygen, abilities, inventory); world (currentMapId, activeCats[], hiddenTerrain[], resourceNodes[]); session (totalPlaytimeMs, isSwimming, isDiving).

Existing convenience methods (addYarn, deductYarn, onYarnChange, addResource, hasInventorySpace, onInventoryChange) preserved as wrappers delegating to the new path-based store internally. All existing callers continue to work without changes.
set() triggers registered onChange() callbacks for that path.
serialize() excludes transient state (session data, debug overrides) and returns a JSON-safe object matching the SaveData shape.
restore(data) hydrates all persistable state from a saved snapshot and triggers change callbacks for each restored path.
State validation: set() rejects invalid values (negative health, yarn below 0, level out of range).
isDirty flag: set to true by set() (used later by Persistence for auto-save). Exposed as a public getter.

Tests in src/game/__tests__/engine/GameState.test.ts cover: get/set round-trip, onChange fires with old+new values, serialize excludes session, restore hydrates and fires callbacks, validation rejects bad values, isDirty tracking.
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: GameState already exists at src/game/engine/GameState.ts with yarn + inventory tracking. EXTEND it -- do not create a new file at src/game/state/. Keep all existing methods working.

userStories.title: Save data format & versioning
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a versioned save format so future schema changes don't break existing saves.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/state/SaveData.ts defines a SaveData TypeScript interface and a saveDataSchema Zod schema for runtime validation: { version: string, character: { appearance, stats, inventory, position }, world: { activeCats, resourceNodeCooldowns }, session: { totalPlaytimeMs } }.
src/game/state/migrations.ts exports migrateIfNeeded(data: unknown): SaveData.

migrateIfNeeded behavior: if version === CURRENT_VERSION ('0.1') -> validate with Zod and return. If version is older -> chain migration transforms. If version is unknown/future -> throw SaveMigrationError with user-presentable message.
Stub migration '0.1' -> '0.2' included as a template (transform function that is a no-op since '0.2' doesn't exist yet, but shows the pattern).
The saveDataSchema is also imported by gameRouter.upsertSave (src/server/api/routers/game.ts) to validate the saveData input. Replace the current Record<string, unknown> with proper Zod validation.
CURRENT_VERSION constant exported from SaveData.ts for use by Persistence and migrations.

Tests in src/game/__tests__/engine/SaveData.test.ts cover: valid data passes schema, invalid data rejected, migrateIfNeeded no-ops on current version, migrateIfNeeded throws on unknown version, migration chain works for stub 0.1->0.2.
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: Create new src/game/state/ directory for SaveData.ts and migrations.ts. The Zod schema must be importable from both client (game engine) and server (gameRouter) -- no browser-only or server-only imports in the schema module.

userStories.title: Persistence module (save/load/auto-save)
userStories.description: As a player, I want my game to auto-save so I never lose progress.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/state/Persistence.ts exposes: save(): Promise<void>, load(): Promise<SaveData | null>, startAutoSave(intervalMs: number), stopAutoSave(), forceSave(): Promise<void>.
Constructor takes { gameState: GameState, trpcAdapter: GameTrpcAdapter, eventBus: EventBus }.
save(): calls gameState.serialize(), validates with saveDataSchema Zod guard, sends via trpcAdapter.upsertSave({ version: CURRENT_VERSION, saveData }).
load(): calls trpcAdapter.getSave(), if null returns null, otherwise runs migrateIfNeeded() and returns SaveData.

GameTrpcAdapter interface (in GameCanvas.tsx or a shared types file) extended with getSave(): Promise<{ version: string, saveData: Record<string, unknown> } | null>. GameCanvas creates the adapter using the existing tRPC hooks.
startAutoSave(30000): sets an interval that calls save() only if gameState.isDirty is true.
Dirty tracking: gameState.isDirty set by GameState.set(), cleared by Persistence after successful save.
Position-specific dirty throttle: position changes only set isDirty every 5 seconds (Persistence tracks lastPositionDirtyAt). Other state changes mark dirty immediately.
forceSave(): immediate save regardless of isDirty -- used by manual save button.
isSaving: boolean and lastSavedAt: number | null exposed as public getters for HUD consumption.
Save errors caught and emitted as SAVE_FAILED events on EventBus with the error message. Never crash the game loop.
stopAutoSave(): clears the interval timer. Called on game dispose.

Tests in src/game/__tests__/engine/Persistence.test.ts: mock trpcAdapter (vi.fn()), test save serializes+validates+sends, test load fetches+migrates, test auto-save skips when clean, test auto-save fires when dirty, test forceSave ignores dirty flag, test save error emits SAVE_FAILED, test position dirty throttle.
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: This story does NOT include the beacon-save route or beforeunload handler -- those are in US-204. Focus on the core persistence engine.

userStories.title: Beacon save endpoint + beforeunload handler
userStories.description: As a player, I want my progress saved even when I close the browser tab.
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/api/game/beacon-save/route.ts: POST handler that resolves WorkOS session via withAuth(), validates body with saveDataSchema, calls the same Drizzle upsert logic as gameRouter.upsertSave.

Extract the save-upsert DB logic from gameRouter into a shared helper (e.g., src/server/game/upsertGameSave.ts) so gameRouter.upsertSave and beacon-save/route.ts both call it. This prevents drift between the two save paths.
Persistence.ts adds setupBeforeUnload() called during initialization: registers window.beforeunload handler that calls navigator.sendBeacon('/api/game/beacon-save', JSON.stringify(serializedState)).
If navigator.sendBeacon is unavailable (some browsers in private mode), fall back gracefully -- no error thrown, just skip.
Beacon payload includes { version, saveData } matching the same shape as upsertSave input.
Persistence.dispose() removes the beforeunload listener.
Tests: test beacon route handler with mocked session + valid/invalid payloads, test setupBeforeUnload registers/unregisters listener, test sendBeacon fallback when unavailable.
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: The shared upsert helper must accept userId + version + saveData and handle the Drizzle onConflictDoUpdate. withAuth() pattern: see existing protected routes for reference.

userStories.title: Load & restore flow
userStories.description: As a returning player, I want to resume where I left off when I open the game.
acceptanceCriteria: On /play mount (in GameCanvas.tsx, after auth + character check): Persistence.load() is called before game.start().
If no save exists AND no character: redirect to Character Creator (existing flow from PRD-01).
If character exists but no save: start new game at map spawn point with default state (10 yarn, full health).
If save exists: gameState.restore(saveData) called before spawning player -> player entity at saved position with saved stats, inventory, yarn.
Active cats from save: re-summoned at saved positions. Validate each position against terrain via MapManager.getTerrainAt() -- invalid positions silently skip (cat not re-summoned).
Resource node cooldowns from save: restored to ResourceNode components (mid-cooldown nodes stay mid-cooldown with remaining time calculated from save timestamp).
Loading state: GameCanvas shows a loading indicator while Persistence.load() is in flight (before game renders).
Error handling: if load fails (network error / TRPCError), show retry button + 'Start New Game' option. Never silently fall back to default -- user must consent to losing their save.
Auto-save started after successful load/restore via Persistence.startAutoSave(config.autoSaveIntervalMs).
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: load page, confirm loading state appears, game starts with correct state.
userStories.notes: This story integrates Persistence into the existing GameCanvas lifecycle. The loading/error UI can be simple DOM overlays -- no need for a full React component since GameCanvas manages its own DOM.

userStories.title: Debug Menu -- Player tab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to modify player stats in real-time so I can test different progression states.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/ui/DebugMenu.ts: class that creates a tabbed panel UI. Constructor gated with if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'development') return -- ensures tree-shaking in production bundles.
Ctrl+D toggles menu open/closed. Only registers the keyboard listener in development mode.
Semi-transparent dark panel (rgba(0,0,0,0.85)) overlaying the game canvas with high z-index. Does NOT pause the game.
Tab bar at top: Player | Cats | World | Session. Player tab active by default. Only Player tab implemented in this story -- other tabs show 'Coming soon' placeholder.

Player tab controls: Level selector (number input 0-10), Health setter (range slider 0 to maxHealth), Yarn setter (number input 0-99), Teleport (X and Z number inputs + 'Teleport' button), Speed multiplier (range slider 0.5x-3.0x modifying runtimeConfig.movement.walkSpeed), God mode toggle (checkbox).
All changes apply immediately via GameState.set() or direct runtimeConfig mutation.
Changes emit DEBUG_VALUE_CHANGED events on EventBus with { key, value } payload.
Debug overrides stored in a transient debugState object on DebugMenu -- never included in GameState.serialize().
Constructor takes { gameState, eventBus, runtimeConfig, world } dependencies.
dispose() removes all DOM elements and event listeners.
Tests: test debug value changes propagate to GameState, test debug state excluded from serialize(), test toggle open/close state.
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: Ctrl+D opens menu, change player stats, confirm changes apply in-game.
userStories.notes: This story creates the DebugMenu framework (panel, tabs, toggle) AND the Player tab. Other tabs are placeholders -- US-207/208/209 fill them in.

userStories.title: Debug Menu -- Cats tab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to test cat abilities without resource constraints so I can iterate on cat behavior rapidly.
acceptanceCriteria: Cats tab in DebugMenu contains: Force-summon buttons (one per cat type from CAT_REGISTRY, ignores yarn cost and cat limit), Yarn override (number input sets yarn to any amount via GameState.set()), Cat limit override (number input 1-10), Dismiss all button (dismisses all active cats via CatCompanionManager), Cat behavior state display (shows state Idle/Active/Expired for each active cat, updated each frame).
Force-summoned cats appear at player's current position + 2 units offset in X.
All changes apply immediately.
Constructor extended to accept catCompanionManager dependency.
Tests: test force-summon creates cat entity, test dismiss all clears active cats, test yarn override sets GameState yarn.
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open Cats tab, force-summon each type, verify cats appear, dismiss all.
userStories.notes: Depends on DebugMenu framework from US-206. Replace the 'Coming soon' placeholder in the Cats tab.

userStories.title: Debug Menu -- World tab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to manipulate world state so I can test edge cases and balance.
acceptanceCriteria: World tab in DebugMenu contains: Time scale slider (0.25x-4.0x) stored in runtimeConfig -- affects dt passed to all systems EXCEPT InputManager, Reload map button -> MapManager.loadMap(TestMap), Show collision wireframes toggle, Entity count display (total + breakdown by component type from World.query()), Resource controls ('Fill all nodes' resets all ResourceNode cooldowns, 'Empty all nodes' sets max cooldown), Spawn resource node (type dropdown from ResourceType enum + X/Z position inputs + 'Spawn' button).
Time scale: Game.ts multiplies dt by runtimeConfig.timeScale before passing to systems. InputManager receives unscaled dt.
Collision wireframes: SceneManager extended with toggleWireframes(enabled: boolean) that renders collider outlines as a separate Three.js layer.
Constructor extended to accept mapManager, sceneManager dependencies.
Tests: test time scale value propagates to runtimeConfig, test fill/empty all nodes modifies ResourceNode components, test entity count reflects actual world state.
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open World tab, adjust time scale, toggle wireframes, fill/empty nodes.
userStories.notes: Depends on DebugMenu framework from US-206. The wireframe toggle touches SceneManager -- keep the Three.js layer logic minimal (one wireframe material, iterate collider entities).

userStories.title: Debug Menu -- Session tab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to control save/load and view session diagnostics.
acceptanceCriteria: Session tab in DebugMenu contains: Force Save button -> Persistence.forceSave() with success/failure feedback text, Force Load button -> Persistence.load() + gameState.restore() with feedback, Reset All State button -> calls trpcAdapter.deleteSave() and reloads game with default state (keeps character), Last saved timestamp display (updates reactively from Persistence.lastSavedAt), FPS counter (updated each frame from 1/dt), Entity count (World.getEntityCount() or equivalent), Session time (elapsed since game start), Auto-save status ('Enabled (every 30s)' / 'Disabled') with toggle button.
Reset All State shows confirmation dialog ('Are you sure? This deletes your save.') before executing. Uses window.confirm() for simplicity.
Constructor extended to accept persistence dependency.
GameTrpcAdapter interface extended with deleteSave(): Promise<void>. GameCanvas creates the adapter method.
Tests: test force save calls persistence.forceSave(), test reset calls deleteSave, test FPS/entity count display updates.
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open Session tab, verify FPS counter, force save/load, check last saved timestamp.
userStories.notes: Depends on Persistence (US-203) and DebugMenu framework (US-206). The deleteSave adapter method mirrors the pattern established for getSave in US-203.

userStories.title: HUD save indicator
userStories.description: As a player, I want subtle save feedback so I know my progress is being saved without being interrupted.
acceptanceCriteria: HUD.ts extended with a save indicator element: small icon (floppy disk unicode or simple SVG) in the top-right corner of the game canvas.
On successful auto-save: icon appears with a subtle fade-in, shows for 2 seconds, then fades out. CSS transition handles the animation.
On save failure: icon appears with red X overlay, persists for 5 seconds, hover/title attribute shows the error message from the SAVE_FAILED event.
Indicator positioned to not block gameplay or overlap existing HUD elements (hearts, oxygen, inventory).
UIManager.update() receives isSaving and lastSavedAt from Persistence (via HUDState). HUD.setSaveState() method triggers the indicator.
EventBus listener for SAVE_FAILED shows the error variant.
Tests: test save indicator shows on save complete, test error indicator shows on SAVE_FAILED, test indicator hides after timeout.
pnpm test && pnpm typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: trigger a save (via debug menu or auto-save), confirm icon appears and fades.
userStories.notes: Follows existing HUD pattern: build DOM in constructor, update via setter methods. The save/error state comes through the same UIManager.update() pipeline as hearts/oxygen/inventory.
